title: Trade Finance Document Processing: Automate Letters of Credit & Trade Docs
metaTitle: Trade Finance Document Processing - Letters of Credit Automation
metaDescription: Automate processing of letters of credit, bills of lading, and commercial invoices. Convert trade finance PDFs to Excel with AI-powered field extraction.
h1: Trade Finance Document Processing: Letters of Credit & Commercial Documents

hero.headline: Automate Trade Finance Document Processing
hero.subheadline: Convert letters of credit, bills of lading, and commercial invoices from PDF to structured Excel data with AI-powered field extraction

summary: Trade finance operations rely on processing complex documents like letters of credit, bills of lading, and commercial invoices. Manual data entry from these documents creates bottlenecks and increases error rates. AI-powered document processing extracts structured data from trade finance PDFs, converting them to Excel spreadsheets for faster analysis and compliance reporting.
whoItsFor: Trade finance departments processing letters of credit
International trade companies handling shipping documents
Banks and financial institutions managing documentary collections
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing batches of commercial invoices for letter of credit verification
Extracting data from bills of lading for shipment tracking systems
Converting packing lists and certificates of origin to structured formats
supportedInputs: Scanned letters of credit in PDF format
Digital commercial invoices and packing lists
Bills of lading and shipping documents as images or PDFs
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with extracted document fields in separate columns
CSV files ready for import into trade finance management systems
commonChallenges: Manual data entry from complex multi-page letters of credit
Extracting specific fields from various commercial invoice formats
Processing bills of lading with inconsistent layouts across shipping lines
Converting certificate data for compliance reporting requirements
howItWorksSteps: Upload your trade finance PDFs or document images to the platform
Select relevant fields like LC number, beneficiary details, and invoice amounts
AI extracts data using OCR and field recognition optimized for trade documents
Download structured Excel files with extracted data organized in rows and columns
whyPdfExcelAiFits: OCR technology handles scanned trade documents from various sources
Custom field selection accommodates different letter of credit formats
Batch processing capabilities handle multiple documents simultaneously
99%+ accuracy on clear documents reduces manual verification time
limitations: Complex multi-page letters of credit with nested tables may require manual review
Handwritten annotations on shipping documents have limited recognition accuracy
Non-standard document layouts may need custom field configuration

faq.question: Can this process letters of credit from different issuing banks?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI handles various letter of credit formats from different banks, though non-standard layouts may require field customization for optimal extraction accuracy.

faq.question: How does it handle multi-currency commercial invoices?
faq.answer: The system extracts currency symbols and amounts as separate fields, allowing you to process invoices with different currencies in the same batch.

faq.question: What happens with partially filled or amended trade documents?
faq.answer: The AI extracts available data from partially completed documents, but heavily redacted or amended sections may result in missing fields that require manual review.

faq.question: Can it process bills of lading from different shipping companies?
faq.answer: Yes, it handles various bill of lading formats, though accuracy depends on document clarity and standardization of the shipping line's format.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
99%+ accuracy rate on clear trade finance documents
Handles both digital PDFs and scanned document images
Pay-as-you-go pricing with free trial available
exampleUseCases: Converting 50 commercial invoices from a letter of credit presentation into Excel for verification
Extracting shipping data from bills of lading for automated tracking system updates
Processing certificate of origin documents for customs compliance reporting
Converting packing list details into structured format for inventory management
